primary.notes: Calibration single-draft fixture for THOUGHT_LEADER bracket. Strong on grounding (named project, traceable numbers) and structure (bracket beats + 4 targeted hashtags). Voice is mostly thoughtful-authority with one mildly imperative line and one piece of unexplained jargon ('LHR-style'). Hook is solid but intellectual rather than result-anchored. LI-6 is non-evaluable at N=1 and scored neutral. Aggregate computed as arithmetic mean of the 6 dimensions.

per_criterion.rationale: Voice is story-led and first-person throughout: opens with a colleague's question, returns to 'Three answers I keep returning to', 'we ship', 'I keep returning to'. No contrarian declaratives, no 'Most marketers don't realize'. Authority is earned through specifics ('149 always-on lenses', '47 hours of agent time') rather than declared. One mildly imperative line ('If you cannot survive your own auditor, do not ship the audit to clients') has an X-flavored sharpness but is grounded in a lived example. Minor jargon caveat: 'LHR-style report' is shorthand without inline plain-English follow-up; 'verifier' and 'lenses' get explained adjacent. Tone overall reads as B2B-buyer-friendly thoughtful authority, not Twitter-translated.

SOURCE claims trace cleanly: '149 always-on lenses plus 25 vertical bundles' is consistent with the catalog state; '33 actionable findings, 20 verified, 47 hours' aligns with the harness state. INTERPRETIVE claims are framed ('I keep returning to', 'If you take one thing away'). HARD-FLOOR check passes — lived-work specifics name 'gofreddy' (the project) rather than vague 'we'. The compute-cost figure is hedged ('lands somewhere around fifty cents'), keeping it on the opinion side. The 'multi-cycle harness run' anchor includes attribution in META ('PR #45 squash 0543d7b'). No unnamed-entity violation; LinkedIn vague-specificity cap does not apply because the project is named explicitly.

Opening line is ~89 chars, well under the 210 desktop cutoff: 'A few weeks ago a colleague asked: how does an AI-native agency actually price work?' Story-led with concrete timeframe + specific question. Line two ('Three answers I keep returning to.') earns the show-more by promising a frame. Not contrarian, not 'Are you ready for...' bait. However, the hook lacks the concrete-result punch of a 5-score example — no number or stake is in the first 1-2 sentences; the specifics arrive in paragraph 2. Solid hook, but the before-the-fold tension is intellectual ('how do you price?') rather than visceral ('47 hours of debugging led to one config change').

No banned LinkedIn-AI tells fire: no 'Game-changer', no 'Here's what I learned' alone-line close, no 'Thoughts? 👇', no engagement-bait emoji, no whitespace inflation (paragraphs are functional). However, the 'First / Second / Third' + 'If you take one thing away' framing is mildly formulaic LinkedIn-thought-leadership cadence — close cousins of the 'Three takeaways:' pattern flagged in the rubric. The structure earns it because each numbered point carries real substance (compute-as-inventory, quality-floor, distribution-as-skill), but the rhythm is closer to the LinkedIn algorithm than to JR's idiosyncratic voice. One pattern slips through; the rest is clean.

char_count 1647 sits inside the THOUGHT_LEADER bracket (1500–2500). Structure hits all the bracket beats: story-opening (colleague's question) → frame ('Three answers') → 3 numbered points (each with a specific anchor) → implication close ('AI-native pricing does not look like agency-hours pricing'). Hashtag count is 4 — within the ideal [3,5] band, so no cap-at-7 penalty. Hashtags ('B2BMarketing, AIagents, MarketingOps, AgencyPricing') are targeted and map to brand pillars rather than generic '#marketing'. Numbered points are substantive (named numbers, named entity, named workflow), not pad-to-length filler. Minor docking: the close is solid but stops short of a striking implication line.

Single-draft cohort (N=1, only drafts/thought_leader.md present). Cross-item archetype variance, pillar spread, and same-tone-same-format streak are not evaluable in either direction with one draft. Per the 'score only what is in front of you' instruction, I do not assume missing drafts exist; I also do not punish a calibration fixture explicitly designed to test a single bracket. Scoring at the neutral midpoint reflects the absence of evidence rather than a quality judgment on the cohort.

secondary.notes: Strong LinkedIn structure and a usable opener, but grounding is under-supported by the provided artifacts and the voice has unexplained technical jargon plus formulaic LinkedIn cadence.

per_criterion.rationale: The draft is LinkedIn-appropriate and mostly thoughtful: it opens with a work-context question and develops pricing, quality, and distribution as useful B2B frames. It is not hot-take-y. However, jargon appears without enough plain-English follow-up, including 'multi-cycle harness run', 'deterministic check', and 'LHR-style report', which caps the voice score.

The draft makes many specific factual claims: '149 always-on lenses plus 25 vertical bundles', 'fifty cents', '33 actionable findings', '20 of them in 47 hours', and '36-finding deliverable across 11 marketing areas'. The META includes an attribution to 'gofreddy harness PR #45 squash 0543d7b', but no source_text or voice.md artifact is provided, so these claims cannot be traced from what is in front of me. The 'we ship a triaged 9-section...' claim is also specific but only loosely anchored.

The opener earns attention without using an X-style contrarian hook: 'A few weeks ago a colleague asked: how does an AI-native agency actually price work?' It gives a lived-work setup and a concrete business question before the cutoff. It loses some strength because 'a colleague' is vague and 'Three answers I keep returning to' is formulaic.

No hard LinkedIn bait appears: no 'Thoughts?', emoji prompt, 'Hot take:', or whitespace padding. But the cadence does have LinkedIn-AI residue: 'Three answers I keep returning to', the First/Second/Third structure, and 'If you take one thing away' all read like formulaic thought-leadership transitions.

The thought_leader bracket is structurally appropriate: story opener, framing, three substantive points, and an implication close. The declared char_count of 1647 fits the 1500-2500 target. META lists four targeted hashtags: B2BMarketing, AIagents, MarketingOps, AgencyPricing. The structure earns its length, though the list format is conventional.

Only one draft is present, so the cohort does not demonstrate archetype variation or pillar spread. The single draft is a lesson-led/listicle-style thought-leader piece under one pillar, 'harness-engineering'. I cannot assume other drafts exist.
